# devhelm_monitor (Resource)

Manages a DevHelm monitor. Supports HTTP, DNS, TCP, ICMP, Heartbeat, and MCP Server monitor types.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# HTTP monitor — the most common case.
resource "devhelm_monitor" "api" {
  name              = "Public API"
  type              = "HTTP"
  frequency_seconds = 60
  regions           = ["us-east", "eu-west"]

  config = jsonencode({
    url             = "https://api.example.com/health"
    method          = "GET"
    timeout_seconds = 10
  })

  assertions {
    type   = "status_code"
    config = jsonencode({ expected = 200 })
  }

  assertions {
    type   = "response_time"
    config = jsonencode({ threshold_ms = 500 })
  }

  alert_channel_ids = [devhelm_alert_channel.ops.id]
  tag_ids           = [devhelm_tag.production.id]
}

# Heartbeat monitor — for cron jobs and batch workers. The job POSTs to
# `ping_url` after each successful run; if no ping arrives within
# `expectedInterval + gracePeriod`, the monitor fires an incident.
resource "devhelm_monitor" "nightly_etl" {
  name = "Nightly ETL"
  type = "HEARTBEAT"

  config = jsonencode({
    expectedInterval = 86400 # 24h
    gracePeriod      = 3600  # 1h slack for slow runs
  })
}

output "etl_ping_url" {
  description = "Hit this URL from your cron job after a successful run."
  value       = devhelm_monitor.nightly_etl.ping_url
}

# Authenticated HTTP monitor with a secret-backed bearer token. Storing the
# token in `devhelm_secret` keeps it out of state files and CI logs.
resource "devhelm_secret" "api_token" {
  key   = "api_token"
  value = var.api_token # mark as sensitive in your variables.tf
}

resource "devhelm_monitor" "private_api" {
  name              = "Private API"
  type              = "HTTP"
  frequency_seconds = 60

  config = jsonencode({ url = "https://api.example.com/internal/health" })

  auth = jsonencode({
    type  = "bearer"
    token = "{{ secret.api_token }}" # interpolated by the API at probe time
  })
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `config` (String) Monitor configuration as JSON. Shape depends on type (HttpMonitorConfig, DnsMonitorConfig, etc.)
- `name` (String) Human-readable name for this monitor
- `type` (String) Monitor type: HTTP, DNS, TCP, ICMP, HEARTBEAT, or MCP_SERVER

### Optional

- `alert_channel_ids` (List of String) Alert channel IDs to notify on incidents. Omit to preserve the current list; explicitly set to `[]` to clear all channels.
- `assertions` (Block List) Monitor assertions that define pass/fail criteria. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--assertions))
- `auth` (String, Sensitive) Authentication configuration as JSON. Discriminator field `type` selects the auth scheme: `bearer`, `basic`, `header`, or `api_key`. Example: `jsonencode({type = "bearer", token = var.api_token})`
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the monitor is active (default: true)
- `environment_id` (String) Environment ID for variable substitution
- `frequency_seconds` (Number) Check frequency in seconds (30–86400). Server applies its default (60s) when omitted on create. Omit on update to preserve the current value; the API has no way to clear this field.
- `incident_policy` (Attributes) Incident policy controlling when failures escalate to incidents. The API auto-creates a default policy on monitor creation, so omitting this attribute adopts the server defaults; supplying any field overrides the policy in full. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--incident_policy))
- `regions` (List of String) Probe regions (e.g. us-east, eu-west). Omit to preserve the server's current regions; explicitly set to `[]` to clear.
- `tag_ids` (List of String) Tag IDs to attach to this monitor

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Unique identifier for this monitor
- `ping_url` (String) Heartbeat ping URL (only set for HEARTBEAT monitors)

<a id="nestedblock--assertions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `assertions`

Required:

- `config` (String) Assertion configuration as JSON; the inner `type` field is omitted (set via the sibling `type` attribute) and the rest of the shape depends on the assertion kind. Example for `status_code`: `jsonencode({expected = 200})`
- `type` (String) Assertion type discriminator in snake_case wire format (e.g. `status_code`, `response_time`, `body_contains`, `header_value`, `dns_resolves`, `ssl_expiry`, `tcp_connects`). Must match an AssertionType enum value as serialized by the API.

Optional:

- `severity` (String) Assertion severity: fail or warn (default: fail)


<a id="nestedatt--incident_policy"></a>
### Nested Schema for `incident_policy`

Optional:

- `confirmation_type` (String) Confirmation strategy type (e.g. multi_region)
- `consecutive_successes` (Number) Consecutive successes required for recovery
- `cooldown_minutes` (Number) Minutes to wait before auto-resolving
- `max_wait_seconds` (Number) Maximum seconds to wait for multi-region confirmation
- `min_regions_failing` (Number) Minimum regions that must fail to confirm an incident
- `min_regions_passing` (Number) Minimum regions passing for recovery
- `trigger_rules` (Attributes List) Rules that determine when failures escalate to incidents. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--incident_policy--trigger_rules))

<a id="nestedatt--incident_policy--trigger_rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `incident_policy.trigger_rules`

Required:

- `severity` (String) Incident severity: down or degraded
- `type` (String) Rule type: consecutive_failures, failures_in_window, or response_time

Optional:

- `aggregation_type` (String) Aggregation type: all_exceed, average, p95, max
- `count` (Number) Failure count threshold
- `scope` (String) Rule scope: per_region or any_region
- `threshold_ms` (Number) Response time threshold in ms (for response_time)
- `window_minutes` (Number) Time window in minutes (for failures_in_window)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Monitors can be imported by their UUID or by their name. Names must be
# unique within the workspace; ambiguous matches refuse to import.
terraform import devhelm_monitor.api 9b1c2d3e-4f56-7890-abcd-ef1234567890
terraform import devhelm_monitor.api "Public API"
```
